mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day average return in periods with rising industrial production (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day average return in periods with rising initial unemployment claims (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate below 2% signals a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 60-day average return in periods with a federal funds rate below 2% (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening supply environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day average return in periods with rising housing starts (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 60-day average return in periods with rising real disposable personal income (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day average return in periods with rising payroll employment (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day average return in periods with rising real GDP (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index signals a strengthening monetary environment that boosts inflation expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day average return in periods with rising consumer price index (t-test p < 0.05).
